# apm-workspace

> `C:\Users\j138c\.apm` is the working copy of the global APM skill workspace. It manages user-scope skill rollout through `apm.yml`, keeps downloaded upstream sources in `apm_modules/`, and publishes one tracked managed catalog package from `catalog/` as `jey3dayo/apm-workspace/catalog#main`.

Read `README.md` first for the operational model. Read `C:\Users\j138c\.config\docs\tools\apm-workspace.md` for the full contract. Managed authoring happens in `C:\Users\j138c\.config\agents\src\`, not in `apm_modules/`.

## Start Here

- [README](README.md): Human-facing overview, source-of-truth model, daily flow, and maintenance commands.
- [apm.yml](apm.yml): Global dependency manifest. External skills stay here as upstream refs.
- [apm.lock.yaml](apm.lock.yaml): Resolved dependency commits and install state.
- [mise.toml](mise.toml): Workspace task entrypoint.
- [catalog](catalog/): Tracked managed catalog package published as `jey3dayo/apm-workspace/catalog#main`.
- [tests](tests/): Pester regression coverage for helper behavior.

## Important Constraints

- Managed skills use a two-layer model: author in `C:\Users\j138c\.config\agents\src\skills\<id>\`, publish from `C:\Users\j138c\.apm\catalog\.apm\skills\<id>\`.
- Managed shared guidance uses the same model: author in `C:\Users\j138c\.config\agents\src\AGENTS.md`, `agents\**`, `rules\**`, publish from `C:\Users\j138c\.apm\catalog\AGENTS.md`, `agents\**`, `rules\**`.
- Do not treat `apm_modules/` as editable source.
- Do not reintroduce `./packages/*` or `~/.apm/skills/` as the global source of truth.
- After moving a skill into the managed catalog, remove the overlapping external selection from `C:\Users\j138c\.config\nix\agent-skills-sources.nix`.
- Top-level managed `commands/` are intentionally out of scope until `agents/src` gets an authoritative `commands/` tree.

## Default Commands

- `mise run apply`: validate the tracked catalog, install global dependencies, compile Codex output, then sync tracked `AGENTS.md` / `agents/` / `rules/`.
- `mise run doctor`: show target presence, external pin state, overlap count, and catalog health.
- `mise run stage-catalog`: regenerate `catalog/` from the authoring source tree.
- `mise run register-catalog`: install the pushed catalog ref into the global workspace state.
- `mise run validate-catalog`: fail fast when authoring source, tracked catalog, and manifest ref drift.

## Verification

Prefer this sequence after managed-skill changes:

1. `mise run stage-catalog`
2. Commit and push `catalog/`
3. `mise run register-catalog`
4. `mise run doctor`

Healthy output includes `external selection overlap: count=0`, `catalog: ... status=ok`, and target lines with `config=present agents=present rules=present`.
